frameworks.name: world model induction
frameworks.one_line: rapid construction and refinement of internal environment representations via interaction

frameworks.name: static world models in AI
frameworks.one_line: representations learned passively from massive training corpora rather than adaptive interaction

frameworks.name: cognitive science of human adaptation
frameworks.one_line: decades of work on how humans learn and adapt efficiently in novel contexts

frameworks.name: novel-game benchmark paradigm
frameworks.one_line: suites of games with deep continually refreshing structural novelty to test adaptive agents

core_question: How should we evaluate AI agents' capacity for rapid, adaptive world model induction in genuinely novel environments?
key_assumptions: human adaptability stems from efficient construction and refinement of internal world models
world models are best assessed through interactive exploration, not static training
deep structural novelty exposes induction ability that pretraining cannot mask
manipulations: depth and continual refreshing of novelty in game structures
agent interaction and exploration vs static-corpus evaluation
interpretations: current AI evaluations of world models conflate static representation with adaptive induction
novel games with refreshing structure offer an explicit test of rapid world model induction
